verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं युधिष्ठिरो राजा जरासन्धवधं विभो: ।कृष्णस्य चानुभावं तं श्रुत्वा प्रीतस्तमब्रवीत् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ yudhiṣṭhiro rājājarāsandha-vadhaṁ vibhoḥkṛṣṇasya cānubhāvaṁ taṁśrutvā prītas tam abravīt
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having thus heard of the killing of Jarāsandha, and also of almighty Kṛṣṇa’s wonderful power, King Yudhiṣṭhira addressed the Lord as follows with great pleasure.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीयुधिष्ठिर उवाचये स्युस्‍त्रैलोक्यगुरव: सर्वे लोकामहेश्वरा: ।वहन्ति दुर्लभं लब्ध्वा शिरसैवानुशासनम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-yudhiṣṭhira uvācaye syus trai-lokya-guravaḥsarve lokā maheśvarāḥvahanti durlabhaṁ labdvāśirasaivānuśāsanam
verses.text: Śrī Yudhiṣṭhira said: All the exalted spiritual masters of the three worlds, together with the inhabitants and rulers of the various planets, carry on their heads Your command, which is rarely obtained.

verses.sanskrit: स भवानरविन्दाक्षो दीनानामीशमानिनाम् ।धत्तेऽनुशासनं भूमंस्तदत्यन्तविडम्बनम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa bhavān aravindākṣodīnānām īśa-mānināmdhatte ’nuśāsanaṁ bhūmaṁstad atyanta-viḍambanam
verses.text: That You, the lotus-eyed Supreme Lord, accept the orders of wretched fools who presume themselves rulers is a great pretense on Your part, O all-pervading one.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्येकस्याद्वितीयस्य ब्रह्मण: परमात्मन: ।कर्मभिर्वर्धते तेजो ह्रसते च यथा रवे: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy ekasyādvitīyasyabrahmaṇaḥ paramātmanaḥkarmabhir vardhate tejohrasate ca yathā raveḥ
verses.text: But of course the power of the Absolute Truth, the Supreme Soul, the primeval one without a second, is neither increased nor diminished by His activities, any more than the sun’s power is by its movements.

verses.sanskrit: न वै तेऽजित भक्तानां ममाहमिति माधव ।त्वं तवेति च नानाधी: पशूनामिव वैकृती ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: na vai te ’jita bhaktānāṁmamāham iti mādhavatvaṁ taveti ca nānā-dhīḥpaśūnām iva vaikṛtī
verses.text: O unconquerable Mādhava, even Your devotees make no distinctions of “I” and “mine,” “you” and “yours,” for this is the perverted mentality of animals.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइत्युक्त्वा यज्ञिये काले वव्रे युक्तान् स ऋत्विज: ।कृष्णानुमोदित: पार्थो ब्राह्मणान् ब्रह्मवादिन: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaity uktvā yajñiye kālevavre yuktān sa ṛtvijaḥkṛṣṇānumoditaḥ pārthobrāhmaṇān brahma-vādinaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having said this, King Yudhiṣṭhira waited until the proper time for the sacrifice was at hand. Then with Lord Kṛṣṇa’s permission he selected suitable priests, all expert authorities on the Vedas, to execute the sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: द्वैपायनो भरद्वाज: सुमन्तुर्गोतमोऽसित: ।वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: कण्वो मैत्रेय: कवषस्‍त्रित: ॥ ७ ॥विश्वामित्रो वामदेव: सुमतिर्जैमिनि: क्रतु: ।पैल: पराशरो गर्गो वैशम्पायन एव च ॥ ८ ॥अथर्वा कश्यपो धौम्यो रामो भार्गव आसुरि: ।वीतिहोत्रो मधुच्छन्दा वीरसेनोऽकृतव्रण: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dvaipāyano bharadvājaḥsumantur gotamo ’sitaḥvasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ kaṇvomaitreyaḥ kavaṣas tritaḥ
verses.text: He selected Kṛṣṇa-dvaipāyana, Bharadvāja, Sumantu, Gotama and Asita, along with Vasiṣṭha, Cyavana, Kaṇva, Maitreya, Kavaṣa and Trita. He also selected Viśvāmitra, Vāmadeva, Sumati, Jaimini, Kratu, Paila and Parāśara, as well as Garga, Vaiśampāyana, Atharvā, Kaśyapa, Dhaumya, Rāma of the Bhārgavas, Āsuri, Vītihotra, Madhucchandā, Vīrasena and Akṛtavraṇa.

verses.sanskrit: उपहूतास्तथा चान्ये द्रोणभीष्मकृपादय: ।धृतराष्ट्र: सहसुतो विदुरश्च महामति: ॥ १० ॥ब्राह्मणा: क्षत्रिया वैश्या: शूद्रा यज्ञदिद‍ृक्षव: ।तत्रेयु: सर्वराजानो राज्ञां प्रकृतयो नृप ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: upahūtās tathā cānyedroṇa-bhīṣma-kṛpādayaḥdhṛtarāṣṭraḥ saha-sutoviduraś ca mahā-matiḥ
verses.text: O King, others who were invited included Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Kṛpa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra with his sons, the wise Vidura, and many other brāhmaṇas, kṣatriyas, vaiśyas and śūdras, all eager to witness the sacrifice. Indeed, all the kings came there with their entourages.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्ते देवयजनं ब्राह्मणा: स्वर्णलाङ्गलै: ।कृष्ट्वा तत्र यथाम्नायं दीक्षयां चक्रिरे नृपम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas te deva-yajanaṁbrāhmaṇāḥ svarṇa-lāṅgalaiḥkṛṣṭvā tatra yathāmnāyaṁdīkṣayāṁ cakrire nṛpam
verses.text: The brāhmaṇa priests then plowed the sacrificial ground with golden plowshares and initiated King Yudhiṣṭhira for the sacrifice in accordance with the traditions set down by standard authorities.

verses.sanskrit: हैमा: किलोपकरणा वरुणस्य यथा पुरा ।इन्द्रादयो लोकपाला विरिञ्चिभवसंयुता: ॥ १३ ॥सगणा: सिद्धगन्धर्वा विद्याधरमहोरगा: ।मुनयो यक्षरक्षांसि खगकिन्नरचारणा: ॥ १४ ॥राजानश्च समाहूता राजपत्न्‍यश्च सर्वश: ।राजसूयं समीयु: स्म राज्ञ: पाण्डुसुतस्य वै ।मेनिरे कृष्णभक्तस्य सूपपन्नमविस्मिता: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: haimāḥ kilopakaraṇāvaruṇasya yathā purāindrādayo loka-pālāviriñci-bhava-saṁyutāḥ
verses.text: The utensils used in the sacrifice were made of gold, just as in the ancient Rājasūya performed by Lord Varuṇa. Indra, Brahmā, Śiva and many other planetary rulers; the Siddhas and Gandharvas with their entourage; the Vidyādharas; great serpents; sages; Yakṣas; Rākṣasas; celestial birds; Kinnaras; Cāraṇas; and earthly kings — all were invited, and indeed they all came from every direction to the Rājasūya sacrifice of King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu. They were not in the least astonished to see the opulence of the sacrifice, since it was quite appropriate for a devotee of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: अयाजयन् महाराजं याजका देववर्चस: ।राजसूयेन विधिवत् प्रचेतसमिवामरा: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayājayan mahā-rājaṁyājakā deva-varcasaḥrājasūyena vidhi-vatpracetasam ivāmarāḥ
verses.text: The priests, as powerful as gods, performed the Rājasūya sacrifice for King Yudhiṣṭhira in accordance with the Vedic injunctions, just as the demigods had previously performed it for Varuṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सूत्येऽहन्यवनीपालो याजकान् सदसस्पतीन् ।अपूजयन् महाभागान् यथावत् सुसमाहित: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūtye ’hany avanī-pāloyājakān sadasas-patīnapūjayan mahā-bhāgānyathā-vat su-samāhitaḥ
verses.text: On the day of extracting the soma juice, King Yudhiṣṭhira properly and very attentively worshiped the priests and the most exalted personalities of the assembly.

verses.sanskrit: सदस्याग्र्‍यार्हणार्हं वै विमृशन्त: सभासद: ।नाध्यगच्छन्ननैकान्त्यात् सहदेवस्तदाब्रवीत् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sadasyāgryārhaṇārhaṁ vaivimṛśantaḥ sabhā-sadaḥnādhyagacchann anaikāntyātsahadevas tadābravīt
verses.text: The members of the assembly then pondered over who among them should be worshiped first, but since there were many personalities qualified for this honor, they were unable to decide. Finally Sahadeva spoke up.

verses.sanskrit: अर्हति ह्यच्युत: श्रैष्ठ्यं भगवान् सात्वतां पति: ।एष वै देवता: सर्वा देशकालधनादय: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: arhati hy acyutaḥ śraiṣṭhyaṁbhagavān sātvatāṁ patiḥeṣa vai devatāḥ sarvādeśa-kāla-dhanādayaḥ
verses.text: [Sahadeva said:] Certainly it is Acyuta, the Supreme Personality of Godhead and chief of the Yādavas, who deserves the highest position. In truth, He Himself comprises all the demigods worshiped in sacrifice, along with such aspects of the worship as the sacred place, the time and the paraphernalia.

verses.sanskrit: यदात्मकमिदं विश्वं क्रतवश्च यदात्मका: ।अग्निराहुतयो मन्त्रा साङ्ख्यं योगश्च यत्पर: ॥ २० ॥एक एवाद्वितीयोऽसावैतदात्म्यमिदं जगत् ।आत्मनात्माश्रय: सभ्या: सृजत्यवति हन्त्यज: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad-ātmakam idaṁ viśvaṁkratavaś ca yad-ātmakāḥagnir āhutayo mantrāsāṅkhyaṁ yogaś ca yat-paraḥ
verses.text: This entire universe is founded upon Him, as are the great sacrificial performances, with their sacred fires, oblations and mantras. Sāṅkhya and yoga both aim toward Him, the one without a second. O assembly members, that unborn Lord, relying solely on Himself, creates, maintains and destroys this cosmos by His personal energies, and thus the existence of this universe depends on Him alone.

verses.sanskrit: विविधानीह कर्माणि जनयन् यदवेक्षया ।ईहते यदयं सर्व: श्रेयो धर्मादिलक्षणम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: vividhānīha karmāṇijanayan yad-avekṣayāīhate yad ayaṁ sarvaḥśreyo dharmādi-lakṣaṇam
verses.text: He creates the many activities of this world, and thus by His grace the whole world endeavors for the ideals of religiosity, economic development, sense gratification and liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् कृष्णाय महते दीयतां परमार्हणम् ।एवं चेत् सर्वभूतानामात्मनश्चार्हणं भवेत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāt kṛṣṇāya mahatedīyatāṁ paramārhaṇamevaṁ cet sarva-bhūtānāmātmanaś cārhaṇaṁ bhavet
verses.text: Therefore we should give the highest honor to Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Lord. If we do so, we will be honoring all living beings and also our own selves.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभूतात्मभूताय कृष्णायानन्यदर्शिने ।देयं शान्ताय पूर्णाय दत्तस्यानन्त्यमिच्छता ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūtātma-bhūtāyakṛṣṇāyānanya-darśinedeyaṁ śāntāya pūrṇāyadattasyānantyam icchatā
verses.text: Anyone who wishes the honor he gives to be reciprocated infinitely should honor Kṛṣṇa, the perfectly peaceful and perfectly complete Soul of all beings, the Supreme Lord, who views nothing as separate from Himself.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा सहदेवोऽभूत् तूष्णीं कृष्णानुभाववित् ।तच्छ्रुत्वा तुष्टुवु: सर्वे साधु साध्विति सत्तमा: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvā sahadevo ’bhūttūṣṇīṁ kṛṣṇānubhāva-vittac chrutvā tuṣṭuvuḥ sarvesādhu sādhv iti sattamāḥ
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Having said this, Sahadeva, who understood Lord Kṛṣṇa’s powers, fell silent. And having heard his words, all the saintly persons present congratulated him, exclaiming “Excellent! Excellent!”

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा द्विजेरितं राजा ज्ञात्वा हार्दं सभासदाम् ।समर्हयद्‍धृषीकेशं प्रीत: प्रणयविह्वल: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā dvijeritaṁ rājājñātvā hārdaṁ sabhā-sadāmsamarhayad dhṛṣīkeśaṁprītaḥ praṇaya-vihvalaḥ
verses.text: The King was delighted to hear this pronouncement of the brāhmaṇas, from which he understood the mood of the entire assembly. Overwhelmed with love, he fully worshiped Lord Kṛṣṇa, the master of the senses.

verses.sanskrit: तत्पादाववनिज्याप: शिरसा लोकपावनी: ।सभार्य: सानुजामात्य: सकुटुम्बो वहन्मुदा ॥ २७ ॥वासोभि: पीतकौषेयैर्भूषणैश्च महाधनै: ।अर्हयित्वाश्रुपूर्णाक्षो नाशकत् समवेक्षितुम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat-pādāv avanijyāpaḥśirasā loka-pāvanīḥsa-bhāryaḥ sānujāmātyaḥsa-kuṭumbo vahan mudā
verses.text: After bathing Lord Kṛṣṇa’s feet, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira joyfully sprinkled the water upon his own head, and then upon the heads of his wife, brothers, other family members and ministers. That water purifies the whole world. As he honored the Lord with presentations of yellow silken garments and precious jeweled ornaments, the King’s tear-filled eyes prevented him from looking directly at the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं सभाजितं वीक्ष्य सर्वे प्राञ्जलयो जना: ।नमो जयेति नेमुस्तं निपेतु: पुष्पवृष्टय: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ sabhājitaṁ vīkṣyasarve prāñjalayo janāḥnamo jayeti nemus taṁnipetuḥ puṣpa-vṛṣṭayaḥ
verses.text: When they saw Lord Kṛṣṇa thus honored, nearly all who were present joined their palms reverentially, exclaiming “Obeisances to You! All victory to You!” and then bowed down to Him. Flowers rained down from above.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं निशम्य दमघोषसुत: स्वपीठा-दुत्थाय कृष्णगुणवर्णनजातमन्यु: ।उत्क्षिप्य बाहुमिदमाह सदस्यमर्षीसंश्रावयन् भगवते परुषाण्यभीत: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ niśamya damaghoṣa-sutaḥ sva-pīṭhādutthāya kṛṣṇa-guṇa-varṇana-jāta-manyuḥutkṣipya bāhum idam āha sadasy amarṣīsaṁśrāvayan bhagavate paruṣāṇy abhītaḥ
verses.text: The intolerant son of Damaghoṣa became infuriated upon hearing the glorification of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s transcendental qualities. He stood up from his seat and, angrily waving his arms, fearlessly spoke to the entire assembly the following harsh words against the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: ईशो दुरत्यय: काल इति सत्यवती श्रुति: ।वृद्धानामपि यद् बुद्धिर्बालवाक्यैर्विभिद्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: īśo duratyayaḥ kālaiti satyavatī srutiḥvṛddhānām api yad buddhirbāla-vākyair vibhidyate
verses.text: [Śiśupāla said:] The statement of the Vedas that time is the unavoidable controller of all has indeed been proven true, since the intelligence of wise elders has now become diverted by the words of a mere boy.

verses.sanskrit: यूयं पात्रविदां श्रेष्ठा मा मन्ध्वं बालभाषितम् ।सदसस्पतय: सर्वे कृष्णो यत् सम्मतोऽर्हणे ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yūyaṁ pātra-vidāṁ śreṣṭhāmā mandhvaṁ bāla-bhāṣītamsadasas-patayaḥ sarvekṛṣṇo yat sammato ’rhaṇe
verses.text: O leaders of the assembly, you know best who is a fit candidate for being honored. Therefore you should not heed the words of a child when he claims that Kṛṣṇa deserves to be worshiped.

verses.sanskrit: तपोविद्याव्रतधरान् ज्ञानविध्वस्तकल्मषान् ।परमऋषीन्ब्रह्मनिष्ठाल्ँ लोकपालैश्च पूजितान् ॥ ३३ ॥सदस्पतीनतिक्रम्य गोपाल: कुलपांसन: ।यथा काक: पुरोडाशं सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tapo-vidyā-vrata-dharānjñāna-vidhvasta-kalmaṣānparamaṛṣīn brahma-niṣṭhāḻloka-pālaiś ca pūjitān
verses.text: How can you pass over the most exalted members of this assembly — topmost sages dedicated to the Absolute Truth endowed with powers of austerity, divine insight and strict adherence to severe vows, sanctified by knowledge and worshiped even by the rulers of the universe? How does this cowherd boy, the disgrace of His family, deserve your worship, any more than a crow deserves to eat the sacred puroḍāśa rice cake?

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रमकुलापेत: सर्वधर्मबहिष्कृत: ।स्वैरवर्ती गुणैर्हीन: सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: varṇāśrama-kulāpetaḥsarva-dharma-bahiṣ-kṛtaḥsvaira-vartī guṇair hīnaḥsaparyāṁ katham arhati
verses.text: How does one who follows no principles of the social and spiritual orders or of family ethics, who has been excluded from all religious duties, who behaves whimsically, and who has no good qualities — how does such a person deserve to be worshiped?

verses.sanskrit: ययातिनैषां हि कुलं शप्तं सद्भ‍िर्बहिष्कृतम् ।वृथापानरतं शश्वत् सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yayātinaiṣāṁ hi kulaṁśaptaṁ sadbhir bahiṣ-kṛtamvṛthā-pāna-rataṁ śaśvatsaparyāṁ katham arhati
verses.text: Yayāti cursed the dynasty of these Yādavas, and ever since then they have been ostracized by honest men and addicted to liquor. How, then, does Kṛṣṇa deserve to be worshiped?

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मर्षिसेवितान् देशान् हित्वैतेऽब्रह्मवर्चसम् ।समुद्रं दुर्गमाश्रित्य बाधन्ते दस्यव: प्रजा: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahmarṣi-sevitān deśānhitvaite ’brahma-varcasamsamudraṁ durgam āśrityabādhante dasyavaḥ prajāḥ
verses.text: These Yādavas have abandoned the holy lands inhabited by saintly sages and have instead taken shelter of a fortress in the sea, a place where no brahminical principles are observed. There, just like thieves, they harass their subjects.

verses.sanskrit: एवमादीन्यभद्राणि बभाषे नष्टमङ्गल: ।नोवाच किञ्चिद्भगवान्यथा सिंह: शिवारुतम् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evam-ādīny abhadrāṇibabhāṣe naṣṭa-maṅgalaḥnovāca kiñcid bhagavānyathā siṁhaḥ śivā-rutam
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Bereft of all good fortune, Śiśupāla spoke these and other insults. But the Supreme Lord said nothing, just as a lion ignores a jackal’s cry.

verses.sanskrit: भगवन्निन्दनं श्रुत्वा दु:सहं तत् सभासद: ।कर्णौ पिधाय निर्जग्मु: शपन्तश्चेदिपं रुषा ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavan-nindanaṁ śrutvāduḥsahaṁ tat sabhā-sadaḥkarṇau pidhāya nirjagmuḥśapantaś cedi-paṁ ruṣā
verses.text: Upon hearing such intolerable blasphemy of the Lord, several members of the assembly covered their ears and walked out, angrily cursing the King of Cedi.

verses.sanskrit: निन्दां भगवत: श‍ृण्वंस्तत्परस्य जनस्य वा ।ततो नापैति य: सोऽपि यात्यध: सुकृताच्च्युत: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: nindāṁ bhagavataḥ śṛṇvaṁstat-parasya janasya vātato nāpaiti yaḥ so ’piyāty adhaḥ sukṛtāc cyutaḥ
verses.text: Anyone who fails to immediately leave the place where he hears criticism of the Supreme Lord or His faithful devotee will certainly fall down, bereft of his pious credit.

verses.sanskrit: तत: पाण्डुसुता: क्रुद्धा मत्स्यकैकयसृञ्जया: ।उदायुधा: समुत्तस्थु: शिशुपालजिघांसव: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ pāṇḍu-sutāḥ kruddhāmatsya-kaikaya-sṛñjayāḥudāyudhāḥ samuttasthuḥśiśupāla-jighāṁsavaḥ
verses.text: Then the sons of Pāṇḍu became furious, and together with the warriors of the Matsya, Kaikaya and Sṛñjaya clans, they rose up from their seats with weapons poised, ready to kill Śiśupāla.

verses.sanskrit: ततश्चैद्यस्त्वसम्भ्रान्तो जगृहे खड्‍गचर्मणी ।भर्त्सयन् कृष्णपक्षीयान् राज्ञ: सदसि भारत ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataś caidyas tv asambhrāntojagṛhe khaḍga-carmaṇībhartsayan kṛṣṇa-pakṣīyānrājñaḥ sadasi bhārata
verses.text: Undaunted, Śiśupāla then took up his sword and shield in the midst of all the assembled kings, O Bhārata, and hurled insults at those who sided with Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: तावदुत्थाय भगवान् स्वान् निवार्य स्वयं रुषा ।शिर: क्षुरान्तचक्रेण जहारपततो रिपो: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvad utthāya bhagavānsvān nivārya svayaṁ ruṣāśiraḥ kṣurānta-cakreṇajahāra patato ripoḥ
verses.text: At that point the Supreme Lord stood up and checked His devotees. He then angrily sent forth His razor-sharp disc and severed the head of His enemy as he was attacking.

verses.sanskrit: शब्द: कोलाहलोऽथासीच्छिशुपाले हते महान् ।तस्यानुयायिनो भूपा दुद्रुवुर्जीवितैषिण: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śabdaḥ kolāhalo ’thāsīcchiśupāle hate mahāntasyānuyāyino bhūpādudruvur jīvitaiṣiṇaḥ
verses.text: When Śiśupāla was thus killed, a great roar and howl went up from the crowd. Taking advantage of that disturbance, the few kings who were supporters of Śiśupāla quickly left the assembly out of fear for their lives.

verses.sanskrit: चैद्यदेहोत्थितं ज्योतिर्वासुदेवमुपाविशत् ।पश्यतां सर्वभूतानामुल्केव भुवि खाच्च्युता ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: caidya-dehotthitaṁ jyotirvāsudevam upāviśatpaśyatāṁ sarva-bhūtānāmulkeva bhuvi khāc cyutā
verses.text: An effulgent light rose from Śiśupāla’s body and, as everyone watched, entered Lord Kṛṣṇa just like a meteor falling from the sky to the earth.

verses.sanskrit: जन्मत्रयानुगुणितवैरसंरब्धया धिया ।ध्यायंस्तन्मयतां यातो भावो हि भवकारणम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: janma-trayānuguṇita-vaira-saṁrabdhayā dhiyādhyāyaṁs tan-mayatāṁ yātobhāvo hi bhava-kāraṇam
verses.text: Obsessed with hatred of Lord Kṛṣṇa throughout three lifetimes, Śiśupāla attained the Lord’s transcendental nature. Indeed, one’s consciousness determines one’s future birth.

verses.sanskrit: ऋत्विग्भ्य: ससदस्येभ्यो दक्षिणां विपुलामदात् ।सर्वान् सम्पूज्य विधिवच्चक्रेऽवभृथमेकराट् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ṛtvigbhyaḥ sa-sadasyebhyodakṣināṁ vipulām adātsarvān sampūjya vidhi-vaccakre ’vabhṛtham eka-rāṭ
verses.text: Emperor Yudhiṣṭhira gave generous gifts to the sacrificial priests and the members of the assembly, properly honoring them all in the manner prescribed by the Vedas. He then took the avabhṛtha bath.

verses.sanskrit: साधयित्वा क्रतु: राज्ञ: कृष्णो योगेश्वरेश्वर: ।उवास कतिचिन्मासान् सुहृद्भ‍िरभियाचित: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sādhayitvā kratuḥ rājñaḥkṛṣṇo yogeśvareśvaraḥuvāsa katicin māsānsuhṛdbhir abhiyācitaḥ
verses.text: Thus Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of all masters of mystic yoga, saw to the successful execution of this great sacrifice on behalf of King Yudhiṣṭhira. Afterwards, the Lord stayed with His intimate friends for a few months at their earnest request.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽनुज्ञाप्य राजानमनिच्छन्तमपीश्वर: ।ययौ सभार्य: सामात्य: स्वपुरं देवकीसुत: ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’nujñāpya rājānamanicchantam apīśvaraḥyayau sa-bhāryaḥ sāmātyaḥsva-puraṁ devakī-sutaḥ
verses.text: Then the Lord, the son of Devakī, took the reluctant permission of the King and returned to His capital with His wives and ministers.

verses.sanskrit: वर्णितं तदुपाख्यानं मया ते बहुविस्तरम् ।वैकुण्ठवासिनोर्जन्म विप्रशापात् पुन: पुन: ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: varṇitaṁ tad upākhyānaṁmayā te bahu-vistaramvaikuṇṭha-vāsinor janmavipra-śāpāt punaḥ punaḥ
verses.text: I have already described to you in detail the history of the two residents of Vaikuṇṭha who had to undergo repeated births in the material world because of being cursed by brāhmaṇas.

verses.sanskrit: राजसूयावभृथ्येन स्‍नातो राजा युधिष्ठिर: ।ब्रह्मक्षत्रसभामध्ये शुशुभे सुरराडिव ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājasūyāvabhṛthyenasnāto rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥbrahma-kṣatra-sabhā-madhyeśuśubhe sura-rāḍ iva
verses.text: Purified in the final, avabhṛthya ritual, which marked the successful completion of the Rājasūya sacrifice, King Yudhiṣṭhira shone among the assembled brāhmaṇas and kṣatriyas like the King of the demigods himself.

verses.sanskrit: राज्ञा सभाजिता: सर्वे सुरमानवखेचरा: ।कृष्णं क्रतुं च शंसन्त: स्वधामानि ययुर्मुदा ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājñā sabhājitāḥ sarvesura-mānava-khecarāḥkṛṣṇaṁ kratuṁ ca śaṁsantaḥsva-dhāmāni yayur mudā
verses.text: The demigods, humans and residents of intermediate heavens, all properly honored by the King, happily set off for their respective domains while singing the praises of Lord Kṛṣṇa and the great sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्योधनमृते पापं कलिं कुरुकुलामयम् ।यो न सेहे श्रियं स्फीतां द‍ृष्ट्वा पाण्डुसुतस्य ताम् ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: duryodhanam ṛte pāpaṁkaliṁ kuru-kulāmayamyo na sehe śrīyaṁ sphītāṁdṛṣṭvā pāṇḍu-sutasya tām
verses.text: [All were satisfied] except sinful Duryodhana, the personification of the age of quarrel and the disease of the Kuru dynasty. He could not bear to see the flourishing opulence of the son of Pāṇḍu.

verses.sanskrit: य इदं कीर्तयेद् विष्णो: कर्म चैद्यवधादिकम् ।राजमोक्षं वितानं च सर्वपापै: प्रमुच्यते ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ya idaṁ kīrtayed viṣṇoḥkarma caidya-vadhādikamrāja-mokṣaṁ vitānaṁ casarva-pāpaiḥ pramucyate
verses.text: One who recites these activities of Lord Viṣṇu, including the killing of Śiśupāla, the deliverance of the kings and the performance of the Rājasūya sacrifice, is freed from all sins.
